title: The fastest-growing AI repos are not apps — they are skills

scenes.voiceover: The fastest-growing AI repos on GitHub right now are not just new apps. They are skills.
scenes.headline: The fastest-growing AI repos are not apps.
scenes.visual: Large kinetic text. Word 'skills' glows in accent green.

scenes.voiceover: Repos like Matt Pocock's engineering skills, Karpathy-inspired Claude guidelines, and Addy Osmani's agent skills are all pointing at the same shift.
scenes.headline: The evidence is in the repo list
scenes.visual: Three GitHub-style repo cards stack vertically with star-count badges.

scenes.voiceover: AI agents are moving from one-off prompts to reusable operating procedures.
scenes.headline: From prompts → operating procedures
scenes.body: Reusable instructions. Repeatable workflows. Better agent behavior.
scenes.visual: Prompt bubble transforms into checklist cards.

scenes.voiceover: A skill tells the agent how to plan, review code, avoid overengineering, write better docs, or follow a repeatable workflow.
scenes.headline: What does a skill do?
scenes.visual: Five compact capability cards arranged in a clean grid.

scenes.voiceover: That matters because the bottleneck is no longer just model intelligence. It is reliability. The bigger pattern is spreading into design, video, security, and finance.
scenes.headline: The bottleneck is reliability
scenes.body: Better models help. Better workflows compound.
scenes.visual: Two-column comparison: Model Intelligence vs Workflow Reliability.

scenes.voiceover: So the thing to watch is not just which AI app trends next. Watch the workflow layer that teaches agents how to work.
scenes.headline: Watch the workflow layer
scenes.body: Follow for practical AI workflow breakdowns.
scenes.visual: Layer diagram: models → agents → skills → artifacts.
